  • archive — Dealing with mass storage systems »

The Archive base class — Mass storage specific¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class archive.Archive(object)¶

The Archive file is the base class for handling files which are held in a mass storage system. It defines behaviour for finding wich files are offline and calculate their size, stage them and extract them.

Public methods¶

Archive(filelist):
Parameters:filelist (sequence) – a list of full paths to files to be accessed

Calls the offline method to hold the list of files to stage. The list of files could be the result of a call to glob.glob:

import glob
import re

print glob.glob('/usr/local/lib/*z*.a')
  --> ['/usr/local/lib/libboost_serialization.a', '/usr/local/lib/libboost_wserialization.a', '/usr/local/lib/libsz.a', '/usr/local/lib/libz.a']

print [x for x in glob.glob('/usr/local/lib/*z*.a') if not re.search('boost',x) ]
  --> ['/usr/local/lib/libsz.a', '/usr/local/lib/libz.a']
stage():

Initiates a stage, the migration of files which are on tape onto a filesystem designed to hold online files.

copy(destination):
Parameters:destination (string) – full path to a directory in a file system where all the files should be copied to

If required, copies files which were just put online using stage to a specified location.

stageCount():

Returns the number of files to stage.

stageSize():

Returns the size of data to be read from tape in megabytes (MB).

Abstract methods¶

do_stage(filelist):
Parameters:filelist (sequence) – list of files to stage

Should implement the staging of a list of files.

offline(filelist):
Parameters:filelist (sequence) – a list of files in the archive

Should return the list of files take out of filelist which are offline.

filesize(filelist):
Parameters:filelist (sequence) – a list of files in the archive

Should return a list of sizes for each file in bytes.

do_copy(filelist,destination):
Parameters:
  • filelist (sequence) – a list of files in the archive
  • destination (string) – path to the destination directory

Should move, link or copy files to the destination given. This is often an optional step.
